behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a deterministic key-derivation function/object boundary problem (canonicalization, ordering, hashing). No real I/O is required to catch real failures like unstable serialization or parameter-order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for the key builder: assert same semantic parameter sets (including different map orderings) produce identical keys, and a large corpus of distinct parameter sets produces distinct keys. Include edge cases (nulls, arrays, unicode, numeric/string ambiguity).

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule.
behaviors.justification: TTL computation is a deterministic mapping from category/rule config to duration. Real failures are usually arithmetic/unit-conversion/default-handling bugs, catchable at function level without infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the TTL calculator with an injected in-memory freshness config (e.g., profiles=300s, catalog=3600s). Verify exact TTL outputs, boundary values, unknown category fallback behavior, and invalid config handling.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is module wiring: memory monitor + config + strategy selector. A pure unit mock of one component can miss miswiring (wrong metric source, threshold interpretation, stale config propagation).
behaviors.test_description: Integration test instantiating the cache manager with real strategy selector and memory-pressure provider implementation (test-controlled readings). Drive memory below and above threshold and assert active strategy transitions LRU->LFU per config, including hysteresis/flip-flop safeguards if implemented.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses cache, DB write path, and invalidation logic. A pure in-process mock can hide SQL mapping/trigger/listener and key-match bugs. Minimum realistic boundary is multi-module integration with a real local PostgreSQL instance.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with local PostgreSQL: seed rows and corresponding cache entries, perform real UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE on target table, then assert only affected-key entries are invalidated (including multi-row updates and transaction commit timing).

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Algorithm correctness matters, but real failures often come from interaction between policy bookkeeping and cache operations (reads/writes/hits/misses across tiers). Integration is the minimum to catch bookkeeping drift not visible in isolated policy mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real cache implementation at fixed capacity: for LRU, execute a controlled access sequence then insert overflow and assert oldest-unused key is evicted; for LFU, control access counts (with deterministic tie-break checks) then overflow and assert lowest-frequency key is evicted.
